stats.fact: GPT-5.4 achieved 75.0% on the OSWorld-Verified benchmark, surpassing the human expert baseline of 72.4% — the first general-purpose model to exceed this threshold.
GPT-5.4 scored 57.7% on SWE-bench Pro (coding), 75% on OSWorld (computer use), and 83% on GDPval (knowledge work across 44 occupations).
The 1M token context window supports up to 1,050,000 input tokens; input pricing doubles beyond 272K tokens (2x input, 1.5x output).
Standard GPT-5.4 API pricing: $2.50 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens. GPT-5.4 Pro costs $30/$180 per million input/output tokens.
GPT-5.4 was officially released on March 5, 2026, combining a 1M-token context window, native computer control, and five reasoning effort levels in one model.
The reasoning.effort parameter (none, low, medium, high, xhigh) controls cost-quality tradeoff; an xhigh request costs 3-5x more than the same request at low.
On GDPval, GPT-5.4 matches or exceeds industry professionals in 83.0% of comparisons across 44 occupations.
GPT-5.4 is the most token-efficient reasoning model to date, using significantly fewer tokens to solve problems compared to GPT-5.2.
